geography.data_source: MCGM ward boundaries (R/Central, R/South, R/North wards mapped to numeric IDs); includes Sanjay Gandhi National Park buffer; area from MCGM Development Plan 2034

demographics.data_source: Census 2011 R-ward Mumbai suburban district; density = population/area (effective density higher excluding SGNP area); household size from NSSO 76th Round Mumbai suburban estimates; literacy from Census 2011; SC/ST from Census 2011

income_profile.data_source: Median from PLFS 2022-23 Mumbai suburban mid-income quintile; distribution close to normal reflecting middle-income residential character; informal rate from NSSO 78th Round; occupations from MCGM economic census for R-ward

commute_profile.data_source: Long commute distance reflects commuter belt character — most residents work in Andheri-BKC-South Mumbai corridor; MCTS 2016 mode share for R-ward TAZs; commute time reflects peak-hour suburban rail overcrowding; metro lines from MMRDA alignment; car ownership from Census 2011

sensitivity_parameters.data_source: Moderate-high fare sensitivity reflecting long commute costs eating into mid-income budgets; institutional trust slightly below average for railways due to chronic overcrowding experience; exam sensitivity from school density in residential belt; Lokniti-CSDS 2024; political data from Borivali/Dahisar Assembly constituency 2024

social_network_parameters.data_source: Higher clustering from housing society-based social networks (Borivali is dominated by cooperative housing societies); moderate connections from residential density; low cross-income bridging reflecting income homogeneity; residential colony dominant per commuter belt character

informal_economy_nodes.data_source: Vendors around Borivali station (both east and west exits); footfall from Western Railway Borivali station boarding data (~600K daily)
IC Colony market — residential area retail; MCGM trade license data for R-ward
Informal economy at SGNP entrance — food stalls, guides, auto stands; footfall from Forest Department visitor records
Dahisar Check Naka commercial cluster at Mumbai city boundary; high commuter traffic; BEST ridership at Dahisar bus depot
Mandpeshwar Road retail cluster; estimated from MCGM market survey data

tier1_agent_archetype_weights.data_source: Middle Income Residential template from AGENT_SPEC.md; Borivali-Dahisar is a classic Mumbai commuter belt residential zone; weights normalized to sum 1.00 (journalist_tier1 trace removed, weight absorbed into formal_sector)

tier2_agent_types.data_source: Tier 2 counts reflecting dense housing society structure of Borivali (high RWA count); MCGM corporator data for R-ward; school density data from education department
